---
title: "A Decision-Centred Modelling Environment"
---

# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This is the online companion to the manuscript:

**A Decision-Centred Modelling Environment for Long-Horizon Planning 
Under Deep Uncertainty: Foundations, Architecture, and Application to 
Industrial Process Heat Decarbonisation**

*Seyed Ahmad Mahmoudi Lahijani*

---

## Quick Navigation {.unnumbered}

| If you want to... | Go to... |
|---|---|
| Understand the framework's big picture | [Context Declaration](front-matter/context-declaration.qmd) |
| Know how to read this document | [Navigation Guide](front-matter/navigation-guide.qmd) |
| Start the conceptual argument | [Module 0: Orientation](manuscript/module-0-orientation.qmd) |
| See what has been built | [Module 6: The Proof of Concept](manuscript/module-6-poc.qmd) |
| Ask the framework a question | [AI Discussion Panel](#ai-panel) |
| Contribute an extension | [Contributing](../CONTRIBUTING.md) |

---

## The Core Finding {.unnumbered}

> When the Edendale electrification pathway is evaluated against a 
> structured ensemble of 64 futures, **23 of those futures** produce 
> grid hosting capacity exceedances that generate regional infrastructure 
> costs invisible from within the site boundary alone. 
> 
> This result — 36 percent of the ensemble revealing a constraint that 
> site-level analysis cannot see — is the gap this framework fills, 
> made analytically visible.

---

## The Central Commitment {.unnumbered}

The framework is organised around a single foundational reversal:

> *The decision makes the boundary; the artefact makes the connection.*

This document is a living resource. Contributions, extensions, and 
critiques are welcomed. See the 
[Community Extension](front-matter/context-declaration.qmd#community-extension) 
section of the Context Declaration.

::: {.callout-note}
## AI-Assisted Engagement {#ai-panel}
Upload this document to your preferred AI assistant to ask 
framework-specific questions. The Context Declaration is written as 
a system prompt for AI agents. The Node Declaration at the start of 
every section tells AI readers what they can skip and what they must 
read.
:::
